title: Battery pack design
abstract: In at least one embodiment, In at least one embodiment, provided is a battery pack for a vehicle, the battery pack including a battery cage comprising ends, wherein at least one of the ends is slidable with respect to an opposing end, battery cells seated within the cage, and wherein the cage comprising a string and pulley system including a plurality of pulleys mounted with the ends at upper and lower portions of the ends, a string connected between two ends of a spring and extending around the pulleys such that the spring tensions the string so as to bias the at least one end of the battery cage toward the opposing end.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A battery pack for a vehicle, the battery pack comprising : a) a battery cage comprising ends, wherein at least one of the ends is slidable with respect to an opposing end; b) battery cells seated within the cage; and c) the cage comprising a string and pulley system comprising : (1) a plurality of pulleys mounted with the ends at upper and lower portions of the ends; (2) a spring having two ends; and (3) a string connected between the ends of the spring and extending around the pulleys such that the spring tensions the string so as to bias the at least one end of the battery cage toward the opposing end. 2. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein the opposing end fixedly secured with the cage while the at least one slidable end is slidably mounted to with the cage. 3. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein the spring and pulley system is adapted to maintain a uniform pressure on the battery cells. 4. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein a coefficient of the spring is such that the battery cells are compressed between the ends of the cage with a pressure within a range of about 15 psi to about 30 psi. 5. The battery pack of Claim 1 further comprising electrical connections between the battery cells, and wherein the electrical connections comprise a ribbon interconnects. 6. The battery pack of Claim 5, wherein the ribbon interconnects comprise KAPTONÂ® covered copper. 7. The battery pack of Claim 1, further comprising electrical connections to the battery cells, and wherein the electrical connections comprise a ribbon interconnects. 8. The battery pack of Claim 7, wherein the ribbon interconnects comprise KAPTONÂ® covered copper. 9. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein the string comprises KEVLARÂ®. 10. A battery cage for containing battery cells, the cage comprising: a) two ends with side rail portions connected betweeen the two ends, wherein at least one of the ends is slidable with respect to an opposing end; b) battery rails for seating battery cells within the cage; and c) the cage comprising a string and pulley system comprising : (1) a plurality of pulleys mounted on the ends upper and lower portions of the ends; (2) a spring having two ends; and (3) a string connected between the ends of the spring and extending around the pulleys such that the spring tensions the string to bias the at least one end of the battery cage toward the opposing end. 11. The battery cage of Claim 10, wherein the opposing end fixedly secured with the cage while the at least one slidable end is slidably mounted to with the cage. 12. The battery cage of Claim 10, wherein the spring and pulley system is adapted to maintain a uniform pressure on battery cells therein. 13. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein a coefficient of the spring is such that a pressure within a range of about 15 psi to about 30 psi is applied to battery cells between the ends of the cage. 14. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein a first of the two ends of the cage is fixed with the side rail portions of the cage . 15. The battery pack of Claim 1, wherein a second of the two ends is slidably mounted with the side rail portions.
